name: IoT/Edge Architecture
description: An IoT and edge computing architecture connecting field devices through edge gateways to a cloud backend for data ingestion, processing, and analytics. Designed for high-volume telemetry data with constrained devices at the edge, intermediate processing at gateway nodes, and centralized analytics and storage in the cloud. Security focuses on device identity, secure communication protocols, and data integrity across unreliable networks.

zones.name: field-devices
zones.description: Physical devices deployed in the field with limited compute resources and potentially hostile physical environments. These devices may be tampered with, cloned, or compromised, and must be treated as untrusted until authenticated.

zones.name: edge-gateway
zones.description: Edge gateways that aggregate device telemetry, perform protocol translation (e.g., Modbus to MQTT), local filtering, and buffering. They bridge the gap between constrained device protocols and cloud-ready communication.

zones.name: cloud-ingestion
zones.description: The cloud ingestion layer that receives telemetry from edge gateways, manages device identities, and streams data to downstream processing. Handles device provisioning, authentication, and connection management at scale.

zones.name: analytics
zones.description: The analytics and processing tier that transforms raw telemetry into actionable insights. Includes real-time stream processing, machine learning inference, anomaly detection, and visualization dashboards.

zones.name: data-lake
zones.description: The long-term data persistence layer storing historical telemetry, processed analytics, and audit records. Subject to data retention policies and regulatory compliance requirements.

mermaid_template:
flowchart TB
    subgraph field["Field Devices (Untrusted)"]
        sensor1[Temperature Sensor]
        sensor2[Pressure Sensor]
        actuator[Actuator / PLC]
    end
    subgraph edge["Edge Gateway (Semi-Trusted)"]
        gw1(Edge Gateway A)
        gw2(Edge Gateway B)
        local_broker([Local MQTT Broker])
    end
    subgraph ingestion["Cloud Ingestion (Trusted)"]
        hub(IoT Hub / Message Broker)
        registry(Device Registry)
        provision(Provisioning Service)
    end
    subgraph analytics_tier["Analytics (Trusted)"]
        stream(Stream Processor)
        ml(ML Inference)
        alert(Alerting Engine)
        dashboard(Dashboard)
    end
    subgraph datalake["Data Lake (Highly Trusted)"]
        tsdb[(Time-Series DB)]
        lake[(Data Lake / Cold Storage)]
        warehouse[(Data Warehouse)]
    end
    sensor1 -->|MQTT/TLS| gw1
    sensor2 -->|MQTT/TLS| gw1
    actuator -->|Modbus/TLS| gw2
    gw1 --> local_broker
    gw2 --> local_broker
    local_broker -->|MQTT over TLS| hub
    hub --> registry
    hub --> provision
    hub -->|Event Stream| stream
    stream --> ml
    stream --> alert
    ml --> dashboard
    alert --> dashboard
    stream -->|Write| tsdb
    stream -->|Archive| lake
    ml -->|Results| warehouse
